assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response mentions deprecated API usage but references 1.31→1.32 changes, not 1.30, and doesn't specifically address behavior changes

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes detailed section on admission webhooks with commands to check their configuration and timeout settings

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes resource diagnostics and mentions that GKE 1.32 may have different resource reservations for system components

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check kube-system pod health and look for crashlooping pods

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes the specific command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_duration'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes service mesh diagnostics with commands to check Istio/Anthos Service Mesh components

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: While the response includes commands to check events, it doesn't specifically mention scheduling delays or readiness probe failures

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) at all
